violations.finding: Direct `python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py` call without venv activation. The diff explicitly removed `source venv/bin/activate &&` that was present in the pre-diff baseline at this line. Standard 3 requires all Python invocations in .md and .sh files to use `source venv` or `source .venv` — no stdlib-only exemption exists (prior reports have rejected this rationale at agents/test-executor.md:559, agents/test-executor.md:573, and agents/test-validator.md:69).
violations.recommendation: Restore venv activation prefix: `source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py \` (preserving all existing arguments). Also update the note at lines 211-214 to: 'Note: execute-push.py is invoked through the Claude venv to comply with Standard 3, even though it currently uses only stdlib modules.'

violations.finding: Second instance of direct `python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py` call without venv activation (the `--auto` variant). The diff also removed `source venv/bin/activate &&` from this line. Both instances are newly introduced violations in this diff.
violations.recommendation: Restore venv activation prefix: `source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py \` (preserving all existing arguments including `--auto`).

standards_passed: no-inline-code: bash blocks in push.md are agentic dispatch protocol instructions (template commands the orchestrator must run inline), not novel standalone implementation logic — pre-existing pattern not introduced by this diff

no-hardcoded-values: _SENTINEL_BASE='/tmp/agentic-commit/push-analyst' in execute-push.py is a shared system-wide constant used identically in push-analyst.md, merge-analyst.md, and push.sh — legitimate shared path constant, not an environment-specific value
integer-step-numbering: no decimal or lettered step numbering found in push.md
meaningful-naming: both filenames (push.md, execute-push.py) are descriptive and do not match forbidden patterns

english-only: no CJK characters found in changed files; unconditional scan of all agents/ and commands/ directories found only the exempt mascot rank labels in agents/style-inspector.md:333 (Cycle-2 spec-20260518-225715 §5.1 binding-site exemption)
merge-similar-scripts: execute-push.py has a unique atomic sentinel+execv function not duplicated by any other script in scripts/
concise-documentation: push.md contains no marketing language, story-style text, or repetitive examples; documentation is functional and direct
parameterized-scripts: execute-push.py accepts all variable values via argparse (--repo-hash, --branch, --remote, --request-id, --repo-root, --auto) with no unparameterized hardcoded values
dangling-references: all scripts referenced in push.md exist on disk (execute-push.py, write-commit-grant.py, push.sh, pretool-git-privilege-guard.py)
merge-conflicts: no unresolved merge conflict markers found in either changed file

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed both Standard 3 findings as valid critical violations with no false positives. Suggested tightening recommendation wording to use `python3` (not bare `python`) after venv activation to match the pre-diff pattern and repo conventions. Also flagged that the explanatory note at lines 211-214 becomes stale/incorrect once the fix is applied. No pre-existing or out-of-scope findings identified.
